page: Microsoft Azure D16ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv6 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 16 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The CPU includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

The server is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per vCPU. Storage capabilities include a bundled 945 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance external storage volumes. No GPUs are included in this configuration, and it does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations position the D16ds_v6 in the average performance tier for most compute and memory tasks. It achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1771.0 and a multi-core score of 10861.0. Text processing performance is a notable strength, scoring 2338.0 in Geekbench, which places it in the top 25% tier. In memory bandwidth tests, it recorded 1035.16 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 105.59 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. For lightweight AI workloads, the server demonstrated strong text generation performance using a small 135M LLM model, reaching 292.48 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D16ds_v6 offers a balanced resource density, combining dedicated high-frequency Intel Xeon cores with substantial local storage. This configuration is well-suited for workloads requiring a mix of compute, memory, and fast local disk access. Recommended use cases include relational databases, web serving (delivering 24.09 GB/sec in static web serving benchmarks), software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D16ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv6 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C x86_64 processor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs (8 physical cores with hyper-threading) running at 4.2 GHz, backed by a 260 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 945 GB local disk, with support for Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks place the server in the average tier for general compute, with strong results in text processing and small-model LLM text generation. The balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio and fast local storage make this instance highly cost-efficient for general-purpose workloads, including database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 945 GB local disk, optimized for databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16ds_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, substantial memory, and high-speed local storage for enterprise workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU.
Bundled 945 GB local disk with Premium Storage compatibility.
Strong performance in text processing and small-model LLM text generation.
Suited for relational databases, software compilation, and web serving.
